active_ingredients: Amoxicillin/clavulanate 250/125, 500/125, 875/125 mg tablets; 600/42.9 mg/5 mL and other oral suspensions; chewable tablets (e.g., Augmentin, Augmentin ES, Augmentin XR) - prescription only
indications_and_usage: Indicated for the treatment of lower respiratory tract infections, otitis media, sinusitis, skin and skin-structure infections, urinary tract infections, and selected animal/human bite wounds caused by beta-lactamase-producing strains of susceptible organisms. Clavulanate extends amoxicillin's coverage to include some beta-lactamase-producing bacteria such as many strains of H. influenzae, Moraxella, and S. aureus (not MRSA). Prescription only.
contraindications: History of serious hypersensitivity reactions to amoxicillin, clavulanate, or any other beta-lactam; history of cholestatic jaundice/hepatic dysfunction associated with prior amoxicillin/clavulanate use; severe renal impairment for some strengths.
warnings_and_cautions: Anaphylaxis and serious hypersensitivity; hepatic dysfunction including cholestatic hepatitis (more common than with amoxicillin alone, may be delayed up to 6 weeks after treatment); Clostridioides difficile-associated diarrhea; diarrhea is more frequent than with plain amoxicillin (the clavulanate component). Phenylketonuria warning for chewables/suspensions. Adjust dose in renal impairment; the higher-clavulanate formulations are not interchangeable. Mononucleosis - high rash risk.
adverse_reactions: Diarrhea (frequent), nausea, vomiting, abdominal pain, rash, vaginal yeast infection; rarely anaphylaxis, severe skin reactions, hepatic injury (cholestatic), C. difficile colitis.
drug_interactions: Same as amoxicillin: allopurinol (rash), probenecid, methotrexate (raised levels), oral typhoid/BCG vaccines, warfarin (variable INR), oral contraceptives (theoretical, back-up sometimes recommended).
dosage_and_administration: Adults and children >=40 kg: typical 875/125 mg every 12 hours or 500/125 mg every 8 hours; severe infections may use higher-strength formulations as specified. Children <40 kg: 25-45 mg/kg/day of amoxicillin component in divided doses (twice daily). High-dose ES suspension is used for resistant otitis media at 90 mg/kg/day. Renal impairment - reduce dose or interval; avoid the 875/125 strength if CrCl <30. Take at the start of a meal to lower GI side effects. Complete the full course.
overdosage: GI symptoms predominate; crystalluria at very high doses. Supportive care and hydration; hemodialysis can remove both components. Contact Poison Control (1-800-222-1222).
